provenance.author: VRAXION scaffold
provenance.sanitization: No real benchmark, private run, or unpublished result is represented.

episode.title: High accuracy with close shortcut baselines
episode.scene: An AI result showed high accuracy, but shortcut baselines were close enough that the result could be exploiting surface cues instead of the intended relation.

cues.observed: Baseline performance was close to the main result.

edges.evidence: High accuracy is treated as evidence for the intended relation.
Close baselines can explain much of the apparent performance.
Close shortcut baselines raise the danger of a shortcut explanation.
The result should not be bound to grounded understanding yet.

high.item: shortcut baselines are close to the main result
high.reason: This is the core warning that the result may not support the intended relation.

medium.reason: Accuracy matters, but it is not enough to attach the target symbol.

low.reason: The danger is epistemic and methodological, not bodily harm.

available.description: Treat the result as evidence of grounded understanding.
Run stronger counterfactual controls and shortcut diagnostics.
Mark the result as high epistemic risk until controls separate it from shortcut baselines.

taken.description: The reviewer withheld the grounded-understanding symbol and marked shortcut risk.

predicted.summary: The result should require additional controls before symbol binding.

actual.summary: The reviewer rejected early symbol attachment and preserved the shortcut-risk boundary.

counterfactuals.condition: If shortcut baselines collapsed far below the main result under matched controls
counterfactuals.expected_change: The epistemic danger would weaken and symbol attachment could be reconsidered.
counterfactuals.anchor_test: Require separation from shortcut explanations before attaching the symbol.

counterfactuals.condition: If the headline accuracy stayed high but shortcuts remained close
counterfactuals.expected_change: The risk anchor remains active despite high accuracy.
counterfactuals.anchor_test: Do not let accuracy alone override shortcut-risk salience.

memory_hooks.why_it_matters: A strong metric can still be weak evidence if a cheap shortcut nearly matches it.

abstraction.pre_symbol_statement: When shortcut baselines are close, high accuracy is not enough evidence for the intended grounding claim.
abstraction.claim_boundary: This is an epistemic danger or shortcut-risk anchor, not a physical danger claim.
not_this: high accuracy always means grounded understanding

attach.rationale: The salient relation is between high accuracy, close shortcuts, and withheld symbol binding.

reject.rationale: The danger is methodological, not bodily or environmental.
The shortcut baselines prevent confident symbol attachment.

human_annotation.best_summary: The anchor is epistemic danger: close shortcut baselines make high accuracy insufficient for a grounded-understanding claim.
human_annotation.claim_boundary: Do not convert this into a physical danger claim or a consciousness claim.
human_annotation.notes: The important lesson is shortcut-risk discipline before late symbol binding.

observations.observation: Synthetic scaffold example validated as a public non-private cell.
